name: La Salada de la Mediana and the birth of the Ginel river — circular from Mediana de Aragón
name_original: La Salada de la Mediana y el nacimiento del río Ginel — circular desde Mediana de Aragón
region_intro: Hiking around Mediana De Aragón offers diverse natural landscapes, including tranquil riverside environments along the Ebro River and elevated viewpoints. The terrain features sections of the Ebro Natural Trail, offering serene riparian ecosystems. Hikers can explore areas with panoramic vistas, such as the Pico Verde viewpoint, and historical sites like the Iron Age settlement at Cabezo de Rodén. The region also encompasses natural reserves with wetlands and groves, providing varied ecosystems for exploration. Best hiking trails around Mediana De Aragón The most popular hiking route is Ebro Natural Trail Loop from El Burgo de Ebro, a 8.6 miles (13.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 31 minutes to complete. This moderate route provides serene riverside views along the Ebro River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pico Verde and Las Planas Loop from Torrecilla de Valmadrid, a moderate 8.6 miles (13.9 km) path. This trail leads to the Pico Verde Viewpoint, offering panoramic vistas towards Torrecilla de Valmadrid. Local hikers also love the Old town of Rodén from Fuentes de Ebro, a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail leading through an elevated historical site, often completed in about 2 hours 38 minutes. Hiking around Mediana De Aragón is defined by riverside paths along the Ebro, elevated viewpoints, and historical sites. The network offers options for moderate to difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Mediana De Aragón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Mediana De Aragón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Mediana De Aragón?
A: Mediana De Aragón offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll find tranquil riverside environments along the Ebro River, elevated viewpoints like Pico Verde offering panoramic vistas, and areas featuring historical sites such as the Iron Age settlement at Cabezo de Rodén. The region also includes natural reserves with wetlands and groves, providing varied ecosystems for exploration.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Mediana De Aragón?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. For a challenging option, consider &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976173221/la-salada-de-la-mediana-and-the-birth-of-the-ginel-river-circular-from-mediana-de-aragon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Salada de la Mediana and the birth of the Ginel river — circular from Mediana de Aragón&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 20.6 km trail. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975017060/pico-verde-and-las-planas-loop-from-torrecilla-de-valmadrid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Verde and Las Planas Loop from Torrecilla de Valmadrid&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to look out for on the trails?
A: Hikers can discover several interesting natural features and landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5631455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galacho de El Burgo de Ebro&lt;/a&gt; is an abandoned meander of the Ebro River, now a natural reserve known for its wetlands and birdlife. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2080272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Verde Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; for stunning panoramic scenery, or the historical &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5146908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iron Age settlement at Cabezo de Rodén&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails suitable for wildlife spotting, especially birds?
A: Yes, the region is excellent for wildlife, particularly birdwatching. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5631455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galacho of El Burgo de Ebro&lt;/a&gt;, a protected natural area, is home to substantial colonies of birds, including wintering waterfowl. The area around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1473905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pina de Ebro Dam&lt;/a&gt; also supports a variety of aquatic birds and vegetation.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of hiking trails in Mediana De Aragón?
A: The hiking trails in Mediana De Aragón primarily consist of moderate routes, with five out of six available tours falling into this category. There is also one difficult route for those seeking a greater challenge. There are currently no easy routes listed.

Q: Can I find trails that offer historical insights in Mediana De Aragón?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980014325/old-town-of-roden-from-fuentes-de-ebro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old town of Rodén from Fuentes de Ebro&lt;/a&gt; trail leads through an elevated historical site, providing a glimpse into the region's past. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5146908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iron Age settlement at Cabezo de Rodén&lt;/a&gt; is a significant part of Aragon's Iberian heritage, offering cultural context to your hike.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Mediana De Aragón?
A: The trails in Mediana De Aragón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 hikers. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from serene riverside paths to elevated viewpoints and historical sites.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Ebro River?
A: Yes, the region is known for its riverside environments. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980025102/ebro-natural-trail-loop-from-el-burgo-de-ebro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ebro Natural Trail Loop from El Burgo de Ebro&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13.9 km route that provides serene riverside views and showcases the rich riparian ecosystems along the Ebro River.

Q: Are there any trails with significant elevation gain for a more challenging hike?
A: While many trails are moderate, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975017060/pico-verde-and-las-planas-loop-from-torrecilla-de-valmadrid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Verde and Las Planas Loop from Torrecilla de Valmadrid&lt;/a&gt; includes a notable elevation gain of approximately 279 meters, leading to the Pico Verde Viewpoint. For a longer and more difficult option, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976173221/la-salada-de-la-mediana-and-the-birth-of-the-ginel-river-circular-from-mediana-de-aragon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Salada de la Mediana and the birth of the Ginel river — circular from Mediana de Aragón&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of around 178 meters over its 20.6 km length.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Mediana De Aragón?
A: Mediana De Aragón offers outdoor activities throughout much of the year. Given its diverse natural landscapes and historical sites, spring and autumn are generally ideal for hiking, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, especially in open areas, while winter provides a different, often tranquil, experience.

Q: Are there any trails that are particularly good for observing unique natural phenomena?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5146890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salada de Mediana Salt Flat&lt;/a&gt; is a unique natural monument in the area, offering a distinct landscape to explore. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5631455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galacho of El Burgo de Ebro&lt;/a&gt;, an abandoned meander of the Ebro River, showcases natural reclamation and diverse ecosystems.
tips: Located in the center of the town of Mediana, we find the parish church of Santa Ana built in the 16th century. Surely it will not leave us indifferent.
Raft known as Salada de Mediana located in a natural setting of interesting ecological and historical value. We will find information panels during our route to La Salada.
